title: C.R. Bard & Affiliates Pay $17M for Catheter Kickback Scheme
description: C.R. Bard, Inc. and affiliates Liberator Medical Supply, Liberator Holdings, and Rochester Medical Corporation agreed to pay $17 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they provided free samples and discounts to urology practice groups as illegal kickbacks in exchange for using Bard's proprietary 'Link' prescription form for intermittent catheters. The scheme ran from 2016 through February 2020. A former Bard employee filed the qui tam whistleblower lawsuit.

title: Pfizer (Biohaven) Pays $59.7M for Physician Kickbacks via Speaker Programs on Nurtec ODT
description: Pfizer Inc., on behalf of subsidiary Biohaven Pharmaceutical, agreed to pay $59,746,277 to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. From March 2020 through September 2022, Biohaven paid physician speaker honoraria and provided lavish meals at high-end restaurants to induce prescriptions of the migraine drug Nurtec ODT. Programs included attendees with no legitimate educational need such as family members, and some prescribers attended duplicate sessions with no educational value.

title: Jeffrey King (Apex Medical) Pleads Guilty to $1.2B Wound Graft Medicare Fraud
description: Jeffrey King, 46, of Phoenix, Arizona, co-owner of Apex Medical LLC and Viking Medical Consultants LLC, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a $1.2 billion scheme involving medically unnecessary amniotic wound grafts applied to elderly and hospice patients. King and co-conspirators instructed untrained sales reps to order grafts in maximum sizes regardless of wound size to maximize reimbursement. They submitted $1,212,005,778 in false claims — over $960 million targeting Medicare, TRICARE, and CHAMPVA.

title: California Man Pleads Guilty to $17M Sham Hospice and Home Health Medicare Fraud
description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 43, of Granada Hills, California, pleaded guilty to health care fraud, aggravated identity theft, and money laundering for orchestrating a years-long scheme to defraud Medicare of more than $17 million through sham hospice companies and a home health care agency. Fichidzhyan and co-schemers used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported owners of the hospices and submitted false claims for services not provided. He also wrongfully obtained over $1 million for a home health agency by fraudulently using a doctor's identity.

title: Medisca Inc. Pays $21.75M for Inflated AWPs on Compounded Prescription Ingredients
description: Compound ingredient supplier Medisca Inc. agreed to pay $21.75 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly inflated Average Wholesale Prices (AWPs) for two compound prescription ingredients — resveratrol and mometasone furoate — to increase reimbursements its pharmacy customers received from federal health care programs including TRICARE and DOL Workers' Compensation. Medisca bought resveratrol for ~$0.37/gram and reported an AWP of $777/gram. A compounding pharmacist whistleblower will receive $3.4 million.

title: Saint Vincent's Catholic Medical Centers (NY) Pays $29M for Retaining Military Healthcare Overpayments
description: SVCMC Inc. (formerly Saint Vincents Catholic Medical Centers of New York) agreed to pay $29 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly retained erroneously inflated payments from the Department of Defense for healthcare services provided to retired military members and their families. Saint Vincent's allegedly learned of calculation errors resulting in overpayments in 2012 but failed to notify the government or repay the funds, continuing to submit invoices at the inflated rates.

title: Health Net Federal Services & Centene Pay $11.25M for Cybersecurity False Claims (TRICARE)
description: Health Net Federal Services LLC and its parent Centene Corporation agreed to pay $11,253,400 to resolve False Claims Act allegations stemming from cybersecurity failures. From 2015 through 2018, HNFS allegedly failed to meet cybersecurity controls and falsely certified compliance in annual reports to the Defense Health Agency under its TRICARE contract, including failing to timely scan for known vulnerabilities and remediate security flaws on its networks.

title: DMERx VP Gregory Schreck Pleads Guilty to $1B Medicare Fraud Conspiracy
description: Gregory Schreck, 50, Vice President of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud. Schreck admitted he and co-conspirators used the DMERx internet platform to generate false doctors' orders, connecting DME suppliers and pharmacies with telemedicine companies that accepted illegal kickbacks in exchange for signed orders. The DME suppliers and pharmacies paid kickbacks and billed Medicare and other insurers more than $1 billion, of which Medicare paid more than $360 million.

title: Three Convicted for $8M Medicare/Medicaid Fraud Through Kentucky Opioid Addiction Clinics
description: A federal jury convicted Dr. José Alzadon, Michael Bregenzer, and Barbie Vanhoose for their roles in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare and Kentucky Medicaid for over $8 million through Kentucky Addiction Centers, operating clinics in Winchester, Paducah, Paintsville, and London. Each defendant was convicted of conspiracy to commit health care fraud, eight counts of health care fraud, and conspiracy to distribute controlled substances.

title: Seoul Medical Group Pays $62.85M for Medicare Advantage Diagnosis Upcoding
description: Seoul Medical Group, its subsidiary Advanced Medical Management, the former president, and an affiliated radiology group collectively agreed to pay over $62.85 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they fraudulently increased Medicare Advantage reimbursements by submitting unsupported diagnosis codes for severe spinal conditions for patients who did not have those conditions.

title: Diopsys Inc. Pays Up to $14.25M for Off-Label Vision Device Medicare Fraud
description: Diopsys Inc. agreed to pay up to $14.25 million to resolve False Claims Act and state law allegations that the company caused healthcare providers to submit false claims to Medicare and Medicaid for its NOVA device used for electroretinography testing — an indication for which the device lacked FDA clearance — between January 2015 and December 2021. The case arose from a qui tam suit by a California ophthalmologist.

title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Vohra Wound Physicians for Medicare Overbilling in Nursing Homes
description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against Vohra Wound Physicians Management LLC, its founder Dr. Ameet Vohra, and VHS Holdings PA. The complaint alleges Vohra engaged in a nationwide scheme to bill Medicare for medically unnecessary and upcoded wound care services in skilled nursing facilities, using proprietary software that automatically billed higher-reimbursed surgical debridements and corporate revenue quotas that pressured physicians to perform unnecessary procedures.

title: Walgreens Agrees to Pay Up to $350M for Illegally Filling Opioid Prescriptions and False Medicare Claims
description: The Department of Justice, DEA, and HHS-OIG announced that Walgreens Boots Alliance agreed to pay up to $350 million to resolve allegations that Walgreens knowingly filled millions of unlawful controlled substance prescriptions from August 2012 through March 2023 and submitted false claims to federal health care programs. The government alleged Walgreens pressured pharmacists to fill prescriptions quickly without proper verification. The settlement includes a seven-year DEA compliance agreement and a five-year HHS-OIG Corporate Integrity Agreement.

title: Gilead Sciences Pays $202M for Illegal Speaker Program Kickbacks on HIV Drugs
description: The U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York announced a $202 million settlement with Gilead Sciences to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. Between 2011 and 2017, Gilead paid 548 healthcare providers more than $23.7 million in honoraria, meals, and travel through sham speaker programs to induce prescriptions of HIV medications. The settlement provides $49 million for Medicaid programs nationwide, with the remainder going to Medicare, TRICARE, and the AIDS Drug Assistance Program.

title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Aetna, Humana, Elevance & Brokers for $100Ms in Medicare Advantage Kickbacks
description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against three of the nation's largest Medicare Advantage insurers — Aetna, Elevance Health (formerly Anthem), and Humana — and three major brokers — eHealth, GoHealth, and SelectQuote — alleging they paid and received hundreds of millions of dollars in illegal kickbacks for Medicare Advantage enrollment steering from 2016 through at least 2021. Aetna and Humana also allegedly conspired to discriminate against Medicare beneficiaries with disabilities, pressuring brokers to enroll fewer disabled beneficiaries perceived as less profitable.

title: California Hospice Fraudster Petros Fichidzhyan Sentenced to 12 Years for $17M Medicare Fraud
description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 44, of Granada Hills, California was sentenced to 12 years in prison and ordered to pay $17,129,060 in restitution for operating sham hospice companies and a fraudulent home health care agency that defrauded Medicare of more than $17 million. Fichidzhyan and co-conspirators used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported hospice owners and billed for services not provided. Two homes purchased with fraud proceeds were forfeited.

title: DOJ Criminal Division Names Health Care Fraud Top Enforcement Priority in New White-Collar Plan
description: DOJ's Criminal Division head Matthew Galeotti announced the department's first-ever White-Collar Enforcement Plan, explicitly naming 'waste, fraud, and abuse, including healthcare fraud and federal program fraud' as the number one enforcement priority. The plan also updated the Corporate Enforcement Policy, revised monitor selection guidance, and expanded the DOJ Whistleblower Pilot Program to cover tips about health care fraud involving public health care programs and fraud against patients and private insurers.

title: Fresno Community Hospital Pays $31.5M for Anti-Kickback and Stark Law Violations
description: Fresno Community Hospital and Medical Center and its technology affiliate Physicians Network Advantage Inc. agreed to pay $31.5 million and enter a five-year Corporate Integrity Agreement to resolve False Claims Act, Anti-Kickback Statute, and Stark Law allegations. The hospital allegedly provided improper inducements to referring physicians including meals, alcohol, cigars, and substantial EHR software subsidies beginning in 2013. A whistleblower will receive approximately $5 million.

title: Cuban National Julian Lopez Sentenced for $3.2M Miami Medicare DME Fraud
description: Julian Lopez, 55, a Cuban citizen residing in Miami-Dade County, Florida, was sentenced to 30 months in prison and ordered to pay $1.5 million in restitution for his role in a $3.2 million Medicare fraud scheme. Lopez obtained Medicare beneficiary identification cards and sold the personal information to a durable medical equipment company, which billed Medicare for orthotic braces never provided to beneficiaries.

title: Massachusetts DME Owner Raju Sharma Pleads Guilty to $30M Medicare Telemedicine Fraud
description: Raju Sharma, 61, of Sharon, Massachusetts, owner of Pharmagears LLC and RR Medco LLC, agreed to plead guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a nearly $30 million fraud scheme. Between February 2021 and February 2025, Sharma contracted with telemarketing companies to contact Medicare beneficiaries and generate orders for medically unnecessary orthotics via brief telemedicine encounters. His companies billed Medicare approximately $29.6 million and were paid approximately $15.8 million. Sharma used proceeds to purchase three Rolex watches, two Ferraris, and a Mercedes-Benz.

title: CMS Expands Medicare Advantage RADV Audits to All ~550 Plans; Grows Auditor Workforce 50x
description: CMS announced a major expansion of Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audits targeting Medicare Advantage plans, expanding from approximately 60 plans to all eligible MA contracts (approximately 550 plans). Records audited per plan will increase from 35 to between 35 and 200. CMS will grow its medical coding workforce from 40 to approximately 2,000 by September 1, 2025, deploy advanced record-review technology, and complete all outstanding audits for payment years 2018–2024 by early 2026. Federal estimates suggest MA plans may overbill by $17–43 billion annually.

title: Chesapeake Regional Medical Center Indicted for Healthcare Fraud — Rare Hospital Indictment
description: A federal grand jury in the Eastern District of Virginia indicted Chesapeake Regional Medical Center for conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud and obstruction in connection with enabling Dr. Javaid Perwaiz to perform thousands of medically unnecessary surgeries (hysterectomies, elective inductions) from 2010–2019. CRMC allegedly received approximately $18.5 million in reimbursements from Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE for these procedures. This is among the rarest instances of a hospital entity — rather than individuals — being criminally charged for healthcare fraud.
